overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a targeted and professional presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on academic research, computational social science, and disinformation studies. With a combined follower count of 3,580 and an average engagement rate of approximately 5.18%, the social media activity effectively communicates research outcomes, academic opportunities, and conference participation. LinkedIn serves as the primary platform for reaching a large professional audience, while Bluesky shows the highest per-post engagement.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of new papers, software releases, and research grants related to computational social science and disinformation.
Promoting postdoctoral positions and PhD research opportunities within the team.
Sharing participation in academic conferences and engaging in discussions about research methodologies.
Core focus on detecting and studying coordinated inauthentic behavior and information operations across platforms.

recommendations: Maximize cross-promotion of significant announcements (e.g., publications, job openings) across all platforms to leverage different audience demographics and engagement styles. Tailor messaging for each platform (e.g., more formal on LinkedIn, more direct on Bluesky).
Given Bluesky's high engagement rate, consider increasing content output or dedicating more effort to direct interactions (replies, Q&A) on this platform to further build community and amplify reach.

Utilize visual content (e.g., infographics, short videos, project logos) for research announcements and job postings to capture more attention and explain complex topics more accessibly, especially on visually-driven platforms.

Actively engage with comments and questions on all platforms to foster deeper community interaction and provide further details on research or opportunities, turning passive followers into active participants.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest average engagement rate (6.14%) despite having fewer followers than LinkedIn, making it a highly effective platform for generating immediate interaction on new publications and conference participation. Content about research opportunities also performs very well.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon is used for specific academic updates like software releases (CooRnet v2.0) and methodological discussions. It has the smallest follower count among the three platforms but maintains a solid engagement rate (4.55%) with its niche academic audience.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn serves as the platform with the largest professional reach, evident from its highest follower count (1,850). It is particularly effective for formal announcements such as grant awards and job postings, attracting significant likes and comments, indicating its role in professional networking and recruitment.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
